# Keep frontend quality checks out of deployment builds

Linting and TypeScript checking are blocking pull-request QA gates and do not run again inside Vite production delivery builds. The accounts client disables `vite-plugin-eslint` and `vite-plugin-checker` only when Vite's command is `build` in production mode; both plugins remain enabled for the local development server and explicit development-mode builds because their immediate feedback is useful during development. Production and preview builds retain transpilation, asset generation, Sentry source-map upload, and the existing Playwright execution unchanged. Production continues to build from the selected Release tag, and Preview Environment test shards continue to build the pull-request source concurrently rather than introducing a shared build artifact and serial preparation job.
